title: plecostachys serpyllifolia
intro: Plecostachys serpyllifolia, also known as needle-leaf sea lavender or needle-leaved sea lavender, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as an antiseptic. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Plecostachys serpyllifolia are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and nausea.
